DXY.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $100.50 (-1.7%) vs history prev_close $98.83 (-0.1%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: US equities closed modestly lower on Friday, with SPY shedding 0.62% to $662.29 and QQQ down 0.52% to $593.72, extending a week of broad pressure. The S&P 500 remains technically fragile — now 3.3% below its 50-day average and sitting near the 6,500 support zone that has been the key inflection point throughout this drawdown. The dominant story remains the Iran-Hormuz oil crisis, now in its third week per CNBC reporting, with Strait of Hormuz traffic reduced to a trickle and Iran's new Supreme Leader pledging continued blockade of the waterway. The FOMC rate decision on March 19 is the next major catalyst — the Fed meets amid sticky inflation (US 10-year yield at 4.28%, up 0.28% on the day) and an oil shock that complicates the inflation outlook.
commodities_note: WTI crude closed at $98.95, down 0.36% on the day but up dramatically from the $83 lows seen just days ago — per CNBC and Reuters, crude has surged over 20% since the US-Iran conflict began on February 28 as tanker traffic through the Strait collapses. The data shows a -0.36% daily change, consistent with stabilization after the initial spike. Gold is at $5,016.10, down 0.9% on the day but remaining near historically elevated levels — at current prices GLD is well above any reasonable entry zone. The DXY dollar index shows a data conflict: one pipeline source reports a -1.71% daily move (to $98.78) while the historical close series shows a much smaller -0.1% move from $98.83; the -1.71% figure appears to reflect a prior-session baseline discrepancy and should be treated with caution. For context, the dollar remains in the mid-$98-99 range, consistent with recent sessions. No attractive fresh entry exists in gold or oil ETFs at current extended levels — see commodities follow-through note below.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. DBS has stabilised this week (+2.38% over five days) but the one-month picture is still ugly (-3.3%), and at SGD 55.60 it sits 3.4% below a declining 50-day average of SGD 57.56 — meaning the trend has not turned. The banking thesis is being squeezed from both sides: per the SGX Q4 2025 earnings releases, net interest margins are compressing as the rate-pause narrative takes hold, and the logistics cost inflation from the Hormuz crisis is a secondary headwind for trade-finance revenues. The 5.53% dividend yield and 1.1× price-to-book are genuinely attractive in isolation, but the earnings calendar shows results due around April 30 — that binary event, combined with no technical improvement in the 50-day average, argues for patience. Not attractive at current levels; would revisit above SGD 57.60 with volume confirmation or on a pullback to the SGD 52.53 support zone.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. OCBC at SGD 20.70 is just 0.9% below its 50-day average of SGD 20.89 — the closest of the Singapore banks to a clean technical setup — and the 50-day average is actually rising, which separates it from DBS and UOB. However, the macro headwinds that apply to the whole Singapore banking cluster (earnings less on loans versus deposits as rates plateau, STI holding below 5,000) have not resolved. At a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 11.6× and a 4.07% yield it is the most reasonably valued Singapore bank here, but with earnings not until early May and no catalyst to re-rate the sector before then, the risk-reward for a fresh entry is not compelling. Would upgrade to WATCH if OCBC closes above SGD 21.00 on above-average volume, which would confirm a break of the SGD 21.81 resistance zone.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. UOB at SGD 36.15 is 3.0% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.26 and down 7.1% over the past month — the weakest performer in the Singapore bank trio. The 50-day average is rising, which is a faint positive, but RSI at 41 sits in neutral-bearish territory and the SGD 36.17 resistance is virtually at today's price, meaning any recovery attempt hits a ceiling immediately. The next meaningful support below is SGD 34.23. UOB's forward price-to-earnings of 9.9× is the cheapest in the cluster, but cheap multiples have not been a catalyst in a sector facing net interest margin compression. No entry — would watch for a sustained close above SGD 37.26 (the 50-day average) before reconsidering.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — the airline has been pulled into a direct headwind from the Hormuz crisis and was the only Singapore name previously holding a HOLD signal, which is no longer appropriate given the operating environment. Singapore Airlines at SGD 6.53 sits 1.4% below its 50-day average and is down 4.67% over the past month; per the Wall Street Journal, airlines are grappling with disruptions that 'far surpass previous Middle East conflicts,' with jet-fuel costs surging and Gulf-region airspace declared unsafe by European regulators. The forward price-to-earnings of 17.2× is reasonable but offers no margin of safety against a cost shock — fuel is the single biggest operating cost for any carrier. The 5.35% dividend yield is a partial offset, but with earnings not until mid-May and no sign of Hormuz resolution, this is a clear avoid. Revisit if WTI retreats sustainably below $85 or if US Navy escort operations restore meaningful tanker traffic through the Strait.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 57 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from WATCH to CAUTION — the pipeline blocks entry because Singtel is 5.5% above its 50-day average of SGD 4.76 and RSI at 56.7 exceeds the 55 threshold required for a trend exception, so no clean entry exists at current levels. Singtel is the best-performing Singapore name on the watchlist over the past month (+0.8% versus losses of 3-7% for the banks), and the structural story — regional digital infrastructure, fibre rollout, data centre expansion across Southeast Asia — remains intact. The 2.64% yield is modest but the business is operationally defensive. At SGD 5.02 the nearest resistance is SGD 5.15; a pullback toward the SGD 4.76 50-day average would create the first clean entry opportunity. With a forward price-to-earnings of 24.4× the valuation is not stretched for a high-quality infrastructure name if the macro stabilises. No entry at current levels — watch for a pullback to SGD 4.80-4.90.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVIDIA at $180.25 is 2.8% below a declining 50-day average of $185.45, and the 200-day average at $177.37 is now only $2.88 away — that level becomes the key test if selling pressure continues. The Blackwell Ultra ramp is the core thesis: per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Q1 FY2027 revenue is guided at $78 billion, implying Blackwell is shipping ahead of earlier analyst models. But the stock has shed 5.15% over the past month despite that guidance, which speaks to the macro overhang — 10-year yields rising, tariff uncertainty on China exports, and the broader mega-cap concentration unwind narrative that has been running for weeks. RSI at 45 is neutral-to-bearish with no reversal signal yet. Not attractive for fresh entry until NVDA either reclaims the $184-185 zone with volume, or tests and holds the $177 200-day support convincingly.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD is 10.5% below its 50-day average of $216.13 — a deep hole that takes time to climb out of — and the 50-day average is declining, confirming the downtrend. At $193.39 it is approaching the 200-day average of $190.82, which is the next structural support. The unverified Meta GPU supply deal (reportedly MI450-based, per news reports that have not yet been confirmed via SEC filing) would be a transformative catalyst if confirmed, validating AMD as a credible alternative to NVIDIA at hyperscale; that remains speculative until an official announcement. AMD's forward price-to-earnings of 18× is cheap if earnings recover, but with a trailing price-to-earnings of 74× and no near-term earnings catalyst until early May, paying up ahead of confirmation is premature. Earnings about 51 days out. Watch the $190.82 200-day support — a hold there with rising volume would be the first signal worth acting on.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 46x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to CAUTION — Intel's forward price-to-earnings ratio of 46× triggers the pipeline's soft block requiring explicit justification, and while the 18A node story is progressing (per HotHardware, the Arizona fab hit an early production milestone and full-scale production was scheduled to begin Q3 2025), the commercial traction needed to justify a 46× multiple on a turnaround story is not yet visible in the data. At $45.77, Intel is just 0.5% below its 50-day average of $46.01 — technically one of the cleanest setups on the watchlist — and up 5.4% over five days, suggesting some renewed interest. But a 46× forward multiple for a company still in foundry turnaround mode, competing against TSMC with US CHIPS Act funding as the primary thesis catalyst, demands more than production milestones. Earnings are about 39 days out (April 24). The entry case improves materially if Intel closes above $46.97 resistance with volume and confirms customer wins on the 18A node; absent that, the valuation risk outweighs the setup.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML at $1,345.69 is 1.7% below its 50-day average of $1,368.82 — technically close to a clean zone — and up 4.1% over five days, with the 50-day average rising. The long-term thesis around High-NA extreme ultraviolet lithography (the most advanced chip-printing technology, used for next-generation nodes) is intact: per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call, Intel has installed the first commercial tool and customer wallet is growing at a high-teens rate. But the China revenue risk is real — per Seeking Alpha, ASML flagged that China demand will see a slowdown in 2026, and with 100% US tariffs on China now in place, that exposure creates a meaningful headwind. Earnings are 30 days out (April 15), making this a binary event. Forward price-to-earnings of 31.3× is reasonable for a monopoly-position capital equipment supplier but not cheap enough to absorb a China revenue miss. Would watch for a pullback to the $1,320-1,340 zone ahead of the April earnings print.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +9.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), RSI 57 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from BUY to WATCH — Micron is 9.9% above its 50-day average of $387.87 and has only been above that average for 5 sessions (minimum 30 required for a trend exception), so the pipeline's hard block applies and entry is deferred. Micron earnings are in 3 days (March 19) — the same date as the FOMC decision — making this the most time-sensitive binary event on the entire watchlist, and entering any position ahead of a same-day earnings-plus-Fed double event is a setup for unnecessary volatility. The fundamental picture has improved meaningfully: MU is up 15.1% over five days and 3.85% over the past month, trading at a forward price-to-earnings of just 7.8× — genuinely cheap if the memory upcycle is real. MU's CEO has projected memory prices rising approximately 40% through Q2 2026 — a bullish call from a seller, so treat it as directionally useful but not independently verified. High-bandwidth memory demand from AI data centres is the structural driver. After earnings on March 19, if MU pulls back toward the $387-400 zone and holds with volume, that becomes the first clean entry. Watch for entry post-earnings.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon at $207.67 is 7% below a declining 50-day average of $223.31, and the distance to regain the average is meaningful. The $200B AI capital expenditure announcement (per AMZN's Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) is the defining tension here: massive spending is the right long-term move for AWS dominance, but it sent shares down sharply because Q1 operating income guidance disappointed. At a forward price-to-earnings of 22.2× the valuation is not demanding relative to history, but with the 50-day average declining and the next support at $196.93, there is no technical case for entry now. The 200-day average at $224.70 is above the 50-day average, confirming the medium-term trend has broken. Earnings in about 46 days. Would revisit on a test of the $196-197 support zone with stabilising volume.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet at $301.46 is 5.5% below a declining 50-day average of $319.01, with the 100-day average at $307.43 as the more immediate resistance to watch. RSI at 40.5 is in neutral-bearish territory with no reversal signal. The TPU v7 AI compute story (per Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote) and solid cloud growth are real, but the market is not rewarding them in this environment — mega-cap concentration unwind, rising 10-year yields, and tariff uncertainty on international operations are collectively compressing the multiple. Forward price-to-earnings of 22.5× is reasonable but not a catalyst by itself. Earnings are 39 days out (April 24). No entry until Alphabet reclaims the $307-309 resistance band, which requires first closing above the 100-day average.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +22.7% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from WATCH to CAUTION — Lumentum is 22.7% above its 50-day average of $507.38, which triggers the pipeline's extreme extension block with no exceptions. At $622.50, LITE has been one of the best performers on the watchlist since its S&P 500 inclusion on March 9 (per S&P Dow Jones Indices), and the reported NVDA investment — reportedly $2 billion into Lumentum for AI optics research and development — would be transformative if confirmed, but this figure has NOT been confirmed via an SEC 8-K filing and must be treated as unverified. The structural story (optical interconnects as the critical bottleneck in AI data centre buildouts) is legitimate, and record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $665.5 million (+65.5% year over year) validates the demand. But at a forward price-to-earnings of 43× and price-to-book of 52.5×, the valuation already prices in substantial execution. Not attractive at current levels — the first meaningful support is $528; a pullback to that zone would be the entry discussion point.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Coherent at $242.76 is 9.1% above its 50-day average of $222.58, which falls in the extended zone — but COHR has been above that average for 131 consecutive sessions with the average rising, and RSI at exactly 50 is neutral, not overbought. The pipeline does not hard-block this at 9.1% extension, leaving it as a WATCH. The S&P 500 inclusion (per S&P Dow Jones Indices, March 9) is the structural catalyst that triggered the move, and the unverified NVDA investment of reportedly $2 billion (no 8-K confirmation yet) would add another layer if confirmed. At a forward price-to-earnings of 33× the valuation is stretched versus history but more defensible than LITE given Coherent's broader product mix across industrial and communications markets. The nearest resistance is $247.15; a close above that level on above-average volume would be constructive. Near-term support sits at $222-224 (the 50-day average band) — a pullback toward $228-235 would be a much better entry. Earnings in about 52 days.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 70 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from HOLD to CAUTION — XLE is 11.2% above its 50-day average of $51.90 and RSI at 70.2 is in overbought territory, meeting both hard-block conditions. At $57.70, XLE has surged on the Hormuz crisis — up nearly 5% over the past month — but the setup for a fresh entry is now the worst on the watchlist from a technical standpoint. The underlying thesis is real: WTI at $98.95, Brent above $100 per barrel for the first time since 2022 per CNBC, and OPEC members Kuwait and Iraq cutting or losing output. But per Barchart reporting, oil prices fell on 'demand destruction' concerns on Friday, suggesting the easy gains from the supply-fear spike are behind us. The nearest resistance is $57.88, barely above Friday's close — no upside buffer exists. Not attractive at current levels. Would reconsider only if XLE pulls back to the $52-53 zone near its 50-day average, which implies approximately a 8-9% decline from current price, around $52-53.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 82x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Palantir at $150.95 is 1.9% below a declining 50-day average of $153.85, technically in the entry zone on distance alone — but a forward price-to-earnings of 82× triggers the pipeline's soft block, requiring explicit justification. That justification exists in outline: per DoD press releases, Palantir holds a $10 billion Army framework ceiling contract (an indefinite delivery / indefinite quantity arrangement — not firm revenue but a spending authorisation) plus a $448 million Navy ShipOS deal; the 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20 billion represents approximately 61% growth year over year. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200 and Daiwa upgraded to Buy with a $180 price target. The defense AI spending surge from the Iran war is an active accelerant. At 82× forward earnings the valuation demands near-perfect execution and continued government contract flow — any miss or contract delay would be painful. Upgrades to WATCH if Palantir closes above the $153-154 50-day average on volume; no entry below that level. Earnings in about 50 days.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. The Vanguard S&P 500 UCITS ETF at $128.06 is 3.3% below a declining 50-day average of $132.40, with RSI at 37.1 in neutral-bearish territory and the 200-day average at $126.77 sitting only $1.29 below — that 200-day level is the critical next test. The underlying index (SPY at $662.29) remains technically fragile: the 50-day average is declining, the March 9 bounce has fully faded, and the 6,500 support zone on the S&P 500 is increasingly in play. FOMC on March 19 is a binary event for broad indices — if the Fed signals a prolonged pause while oil-driven inflation stays elevated, the pressure on rate-sensitive growth stocks intensifies further. No broad index entry until VUAA reclaims $130-131 (the 100-day average) with conviction, or tests and holds the $125.41 support zone.

singapore.summary: All five Singapore names are on CAUTION — the first time the cluster has been uniformly cautious since tracking began. The notable shift is C6L (Singapore Airlines) dropping from HOLD to CAUTION as the Hormuz crisis directly hits its cost structure; the three banks remain pressured by net interest margin compression meeting a rate-pause macro. Singtel (Z74) is the relative standout given positive one-month performance, but its 5.5% extension above the 50-day average blocks entry.
singapore.thesis_status: Deteriorating — no entry opportunities across the cluster
singapore.key_development: C6L downgraded to CAUTION as airline fuel costs surge from the Hormuz crisis, removing the last HOLD in the Singapore cluster.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster has undergone a significant reassessment: INTC drops from BUY to CAUTION on valuation (46× forward price-to-earnings is hard to justify without confirmed customer wins on the 18A node), and MU shifts from BUY to WATCH due to extreme 5-day extension ahead of its March 19 earnings. NVDA and AMD remain on CAUTION, ASML on CAUTION. The mid-cap outperformance thesis is intact but the entry window has narrowed — MU post-earnings is the cleanest upcoming opportunity.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Mixed — structural demand thesis intact but near-term entries blocked by extension or valuation
semiconductors.key_development: MU earnings on March 19 (same day as FOMC) is the single most important event for the semis cluster this week — the print will either validate the memory upcycle or reset expectations.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG remain on CAUTION with declining 50-day averages, and PLTR holds at HOLD with no signal change. The cluster is uniformly cautious-to-defensive — mega-cap multiple compression from rising yields and tariff uncertainty continues. PLTR is the only name with an active upgrade path (close above $153-154 with volume), making it the sole point of interest.
big_tech.thesis_status: Bearish on AMZN/GOOG, cautiously constructive on PLTR with conditions
big_tech.key_development: PLTR's defense AI contracts (Army IDIQ framework ceiling of $10B, Navy $448M ShipOS) provide fundamental support that separates it from the AMZN/GOOG mega-cap selloff.

photonics.summary: LITE moves from WATCH to CAUTION as its 22.7% extension above the 50-day average triggers the extreme extension block — the S&P 500 inclusion and unverified NVDA investment are priced in and then some. COHR stays on WATCH with RSI at a neutral 50 and 131 sessions above a rising 50-day average, making it the only photonics name worth monitoring for entry. The cluster divergence — LITE overextended, COHR manageable — is the key dynamic.
photonics.thesis_status: Diverging — COHR watchable, LITE unapproachable at current extension
photonics.key_development: LITE's block at 22.7% extension means the NVDA investment story (still unverified) needs an 8-K filing confirmation and a meaningful price pullback before the entry discussion restarts.

energy.summary: XLE moves from HOLD to CAUTION as overbought RSI (70.2) and 11.2% extension above the 50-day average make it one of the worst technical setups on the watchlist despite the bullish Hormuz fundamental backdrop. The oil price thesis is real but already fully priced into the ETF at current levels.
energy.thesis_status: Avoid — fundamentals bullish but entry risk extreme
energy.key_development: WTI stabilisation near $99 with G7 strategic reserve release discussions introduces a ceiling on the supply-fear premium, reducing the risk-reward for chasing XLE here.

index.summary: VUAA.L remains on CAUTION with the 200-day average at $126.77 now just $1.29 below Friday's close — that level becomes the line in the sand for whether the S&P 500 ETF stabilises or breaks lower. FOMC on March 19 is the single biggest near-term determinant of direction for the broad index.
index.key_development: The 6,500 S&P 500 support zone and FOMC March 19 decision together form the binary setup for whether VUAA enters an accumulation range or continues its drawdown toward the $118-119 support.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: Direct manufacturer of NVDA's Blackwell chips and AMD's MI-series GPUs — if MU earnings confirm the AI memory upcycle on March 19, TSMC's wafer utilisation story strengthens as the same hyperscaler buildout drives logic demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, bringing it closer to its 50-day average region — MU's March 19 earnings print is the near-term read-through catalyst.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The other major high-bandwidth memory (the premium memory chips used in AI accelerators) supplier alongside MU — MU's earnings on March 19 will give the clearest signal on whether memory pricing is actually recovering at the rate MU's CEO projects.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after MU's March 19 earnings; if MU guides strongly and SK Hynix has pulled back 10-12% from recent highs, the Korean-listed shares become worth adding to the watchlist for a comparative valuation look.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Arista supplies the high-speed networking switches that connect AI GPU clusters — COHR's optical transceiver demand and the AI data centre buildout confirmed by NVDA's Blackwell ramp directly drives Arista's order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Arista pulls back 8% or more from current levels, particularly if a broader tech selloff following the March 19 FOMC decision creates indiscriminate selling across AI infrastructure names.

interconnected.name: ExxonMobil
interconnected.reason: The largest holding in XLE — with XLE blocked from entry at current extension, monitoring XOM individually gives a cleaner read on whether energy-sector gains are concentrated in the major integrated producers or broadly distributed.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if XOM pulls back 7-9% from current levels toward a zone consistent with WTI stabilising in the $88-92 range, rather than chasing the Hormuz spike price.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom AI chip packaging and networking solutions for hyperscalers — directly linked to the same COHR optical interconnect demand theme, and a potential beneficiary if the unverified NVDA investment in photonics is confirmed via 8-K filing.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back 10% or more from current levels, or revisit after COHR's May 7 earnings confirm the optical interconnect demand pipeline.

active_risks: Iran-US conflict blocking Strait of Hormuz — approximately 20% of world oil supply disrupted, now in week 3
US-China 100% tariffs and technology export controls reshaping semiconductor supply chains
Fed rate-pause risk as oil-driven inflation complicates the path back to 2% target
geopolitical.macro_outlook: The Strait of Hormuz disruption is the dominant market transmission mechanism right now — per CNBC, Reuters, and NPR reporting, daily ship traffic through the Strait has collapsed from 138 vessels to just 2, and Iran's new Supreme Leader has pledged continued blockade. With approximately 20% of global oil supply transiting that waterway, even partial restoration of traffic matters enormously for inflation and corporate margins. The Fed now faces a stagflationary squeeze: oil above $99 keeps goods inflation elevated just as rate-pause expectations are already building, making March 19's FOMC decision uniquely difficult. US-China tariff escalation (100% tariffs plus software export bans announced March 11) adds a second inflation vector via supply-chain disruption, particularly for consumer electronics and semiconductors.

base_case.description: Strait of Hormuz remains partially blocked but G7 strategic reserve releases and US Navy escort operations gradually restore 30-50% of normal tanker flow over the next 4-6 weeks. WTI stays in the $90-105 range. FOMC holds rates steady on March 19, signalling willingness to look through the oil-driven inflation spike. Markets stabilise in the $6,400-6,700 range for the S&P 500.
base_case.market_impact: Oil stays elevated but ceases to be a daily shock; energy stocks (XLE) hold current levels without new highs. Tech multiples remain compressed by 4%+ 10-year yields. Semi divergence (mid-cap over mega-cap) persists. Singapore banks remain under pressure from margin compression.
base_case.portfolio_action: Hold current cautious stance across the watchlist. MU post-earnings entry becomes actionable if the stock pulls back toward $390-400. No broad index (VUAA) entry until SPY reclaims 6,600.

optimistic_case.description: US Navy escorts successfully restore commercial tanker flow through the Strait within 2 weeks; Iran backs down from full blockade under G7 pressure and strategic reserve release. WTI falls back toward $80-85. Fed signals resumption of rate cuts at March 19 meeting or in May.
optimistic_case.market_impact: Sharp rally in risk assets — SPY likely reclaims 6,700-6,800. XLE gives back 10-15% of recent gains as oil retreats. Tech names (AMZN, GOOG, NVDA) recover as multiple compression reverses. Singapore banks stabilise on rate-cut signals. VUAA would upgrade to WATCH.
optimistic_case.portfolio_action: If tanker flow resumes and WTI drops below $87 with confirmation, begin building NVDA and AMZN positions at the entries flagged in their writeups. Upgrade VUAA to WATCH. Reduce urgency on XLE energy thesis.

pessimistic_case.description: Iran lays sea mines in the Strait and attacks escort vessels; US military response escalates beyond airstrikes. Oil spikes above $120 again. Fed forced to choose between inflation (arguing for hikes) and recession risk (arguing for cuts), likely pausing indefinitely. S&P 500 breaks below 6,400.
pessimistic_case.market_impact: Oil above $120 is catastrophic for airlines (C6L), consumer discretionary, and logistics margins globally. Tech names (AMZN, GOOG) face double pressure from rate uncertainty plus cost inflation. PLTR and defence names likely outperform. Gold holds near $5,000. VUAA breaks to $118-119 support.
pessimistic_case.portfolio_action: Pause all new entries. PLTR's defence contract thesis becomes the one area worth monitoring for accumulation on any dip below $140. C6L becomes a hard avoid at any price. If SPY breaks $640, revisit gold (GLD) as a portfolio hedge — though current GLD levels remain extended.

wildcard.description: Iran formally closes the Strait by declaring it a war zone and deploying submarine mines, simultaneously triggering a cyber attack on Gulf oil terminal infrastructure. This would be a true supply shock, not just a flow disruption — Saudi Aramco and UAE ADNOC onshore operations would be directly threatened.
wildcard.market_impact: WTI could spike toward $130-140 within days. Global recession probability rises sharply as energy costs destroy demand across manufacturing, aviation, and shipping. Equity markets likely fall 10-15% in a week. Dollar surges on safe-haven flows.
wildcard.portfolio_action: If this scenario materialises, pause all entries across the watchlist. The names most exposed to forced selling are Singapore positions (D05, O39, U11, C6L) and extended technology names (LITE, COHR). PLTR and cash are the only defensible positions — even XLE would likely sell off as recession fears overwhelm the supply shock premium.

events_this_week.event: WTI crude at $98.95 — testing the $100 psychological level as Hormuz crisis enters week 3; Brent already above $100 per CNBC
SPY at $662.29 — approaching the 6,500 support zone that has been the key floor for the current drawdown; 50-day average is declining at $682+
VUAA.L at $128.06 — 200-day average at $126.77 now $1.29 away, representing the next major structural support level
VIX at 27.19 — elevated but not at panic levels; sustained above 25 signals residual geopolitical and macro tail risk
XLE at $57.70 with RSI overbought at 70.2 — energy ETF 11.2% above its 50-day average with no clean entry; overbought conditions warrant caution despite bullish fundamental backdrop
FOMC Rate Decision (2:00 PM ET) — Fed meets with oil above $99 and 10-year yields at 4.28%; any signal of prolonged pause would pressure tech multiples further
Micron (MU) Earnings — memory pricing, high-bandwidth memory demand, and AI datacenter capex outlook are the three key numbers to watch; result determines whether the memory upcycle thesis is real
ASML Earnings — China revenue slowdown in 2026 vs. High-NA EUV ramp from Intel/Samsung will be the key tension; 30 days out
Intel (INTC) Earnings — 18A node customer win announcements and foundry revenue ramp are the specific data points needed to justify the current 46× forward price-to-earnings multiple
Alphabet (GOOG) Earnings — cloud revenue growth rate and TPU v7 monetisation are the key metrics given current mega-cap selloff
DBS (D05.SI) Earnings — net interest margin trajectory and non-performing loan levels will determine whether Singapore banking thesis stabilises or deteriorates further

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — most cautious posture since tracking began, zero BUY signals
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Iran-Hormuz oil crisis entering week 3, combined with FOMC uncertainty on March 19 and a technically broken S&P 500, has pushed every actionable name either into caution territory or blocked by extension — MU post-earnings and COHR on a pullback are the only near-term entry opportunities worth watching.

watch_for_entry.note: Closest to actionable — earnings March 19 is the binary trigger. Post-earnings pullback to $387-400 is the entry zone. Half position if MU guides strongly and macro doesn't deteriorate further.
131 sessions above a rising 50-day average with RSI at neutral 50 — best technical posture of any WATCH name. Entry only on a pullback; current 9% extension is too wide for a clean entry.
